User Story: As a user, I want a persistent sidebar, header with breadcrumbs and bottom nav (mobile) so that I can navigate between all pages.
Expected Behaviour: HashRouter routing; sidebar lists routes grouped by section; header shows breadcrumb of current page; on localhost a 'Demo / Mock Mode' badge is shown; unknown routes redirect to the role-based home (default /staffing).

User Story: As an admin, I want login protection with roles and per-page permissions so that users only see what they may access.
Expected Behaviour: When login protection is enabled, unauthenticated users are redirected to /login; ADMIN always has permission; other roles need route in user.permissions; users with mustChangePassword see a forced password-change screen (min 8 chars, confirm must match). In mock mode protection is disabled.

User Story: As a developer/demo user, I want the app on localhost to use a localStorage-backed mock DB so that I can try everything without a backend.
Expected Behaviour: isLocalPreview() intercepts all /api calls; data persists in localStorage key staffing_planner_local_db_v1; login always succeeds with mock-jwt-token.

User Story: As a user, I want success/error toasts after actions so that I get feedback.
Expected Behaviour: addToast shows dismissible toast; auto-dismiss after timeout; error messages from API surfaced.

User Story: As a user, I want to switch theme and have it persisted so that the UI matches my preference.
Expected Behaviour: ThemeContext persists theme; CSS tokens switch palettes; default follows stored preference.

User Story: As a user, I want a search palette (header/bottom nav) so that I can find entities quickly from anywhere.
Expected Behaviour: SearchWidget portal opens; typing searches across entities; results navigate to entity pages; /global-search page for full results.

User Story: As a planner, I want an editable per-day allocation grid of resources × projects so that I can set daily allocation percentages.
Expected Behaviour: Day view shows 14 days; each assignment row has numeric inputs 0-100 (step 5); values clamp to 0-100; edits are debounced 400ms then persisted; weekend/holiday cells are read-only '-'; full-day approved leave blocks the cell with the leave-type icon/color; half-day leave shows a corner marker but stays editable.

User Story: As a planner, I want weekly/monthly average views so that I can see load over 12 weeks/12 months.
Expected Behaviour: Week/month views show 12 read-only averaged cells per assignment and per resource; colors: red >100% (or >max), teal =100%/max, yellow partial; resource lastDayOfWork caps the effective period.

Feature: Daily total per resource with overload diagnosis
User Story: As a planner, I want a daily total row per resource with color coding and a diagnosis modal so that I understand and fix overloads.
Expected Behaviour: Total cell sums all assignments; red if > maxStaffingPercentage (half-day leave adds 50% if affectsCapacity); clicking a non-zero total opens 'Composizione del carico' modal listing per-project percentages with 0-100% (step5) dropdowns to fix inline; overflow badge shows +X% over max.

User Story: As a planner, I want Prev/Today/Next and Day/Week/Month toggles so that I can move through time.
Expected Behaviour: Prev/Next move by 1 day/7 days/1 month depending on view; 'Oggi' returns to current date; view toggle re-renders columns (14 days / 12 weeks / 12 months).

User Story: As a planner, I want to assign a resource to one or more projects so that allocation rows are created.
Expected Behaviour: 'Assegna Risorsa' opens FormDialog with searchable resource select and multi-select of non-completed projects; submit creates assignments (duplicates skipped server-side); per-resource '+' button pre-selects that resource.

User Story: As a planner, I want to fill a date range with a percentage in one action and undo it so that mass planning is fast and safe.
Expected Behaviour: Calendar_add_on button opens 'Assegnazione Massiva' (startDate, endDate, percentage); submit sets percentage on working days in range; snackbar appears with count and 'Annulla' that restores previous values exactly.

User Story: As a planner, I want to remove an assignment with confirmation so that I don't delete by mistake.
Expected Behaviour: Delete icon opens ConfirmationModal naming resource and project; confirm deletes assignment and its allocations; row disappears.

User Story: As a planner, I want to filter by resource, client, PM and project and paginate so that large teams stay manageable.
Expected Behaviour: Filters are debounced 400ms; filtering by project/client/PM restricts resources to those with matching assignments; 'Reset Filtri' clears all; pagination 20/page default with page-size selector; resigned resources are always excluded.

User Story: As a planner, I want assignments with no allocation in the visible period hidden (unless never populated) so that the grid stays clean.
Expected Behaviour: An assignment with some non-zero allocations only shows when at least one non-zero day falls in the visible range; assignments with no allocations at all always show.

User Story: As a mobile user, I want per-resource cards with load bars and a slider editor so that I can edit staffing on the phone.
Expected Behaviour: Below 768px: cards show name, role, avg load badge and bar, assignment list; tapping an assignment opens a full-screen editor with a 0-100 step-5 slider per day; non-working days are read-only.

User Story: As a planner, I want to export the visible staffing list so that I can share it.
Expected Behaviour: ExportButton exports current page resources with name, role, assigned projects, location, max %, email, tutor.

User Story: As a planner, I want resource and project names linked so that I can jump to their detail pages.
Expected Behaviour: Resource name links to /workload?resourceId=...; project name links to /projects?projectId=....

User Story: As a manager, I want KPI cards organized in configurable tabs so that I can see the analytics that matter to me.
Expected Behaviour: Tabs come from dashboardLayout (UIConfig); first tab is active by default; each tab renders its configured cards in a 2-column grid; full-width cards span both columns.

User Story: As a manager, I want top-level KPIs (total budget, active resources/projects, month cost, person-days) so that I get an instant company pulse.
Expected Behaviour: KpiHeaderCards show overall totals; current-month cost uses historical role cost × allocation × realization%, excluding weekends/holidays.

User Story: As a manager, I want warnings for unassigned resources and unstaffed 'In corso' projects so that I can act.
Expected Behaviour: AttentionCards list unassigned active resources (max%>0) and in-progress projects with no assignments; clicking navigates to the relevant filtered page.

User Story: As a manager, I want per-resource average allocation for this and next month with a resource filter so that I can spot under/over allocation.
Expected Behaviour: Card computes monthly averages from allocations on working days; totals row shows averages; resource filter narrows rows.

User Story: As a manager, I want FTE and person-days per project (client filter) so that I can see effort distribution.
Expected Behaviour: FTE = person-days within project dates / working days of project; projects with 0 days hidden; client filter applies.

User Story: As a manager, I want budget vs estimated cost variance per project so that I can see overruns.
Expected Behaviour: Estimated cost = sum of allocation × historical role cost × realization%; variance = budget - cost; totals row; client filter.

User Story: As a manager, I want pro-rata budget vs cost in a chosen date range so that I can check burn rate.
Expected Behaviour: Period budget = daily budget (budget/working days of project) × working days of overlap with filter range; cost computed in range; client+date filters; invalid dates yield empty list.

User Story: As a manager, I want the average daily cost rate per project in a range so that I can compare project economics.
Expected Behaviour: avgDailyRate = totalCost/totalPersonDays over filter range (default current month); projects with 0 days hidden.

User Story: As a manager, I want resources under 100% average allocation for a selected month so that I can fill capacity.
Expected Behaviour: Month picker (default current); list of resources with avg<100% and their percentage.

Feature: Monthly cost by client / unallocated FTE / leaves overview
User Story: As a manager, I want month cost per client, unallocated FTE and a leaves overview so that I see cost distribution and absences.
Expected Behaviour: Client cost card lists clients with cost>0 (click navigates); UnallocatedFTE = available FTE - allocated FTE this month; LeavesOverview links to /leaves.

User Story: As a manager, I want effort split by function, industry and a function×industry FTE matrix so that I understand where effort goes.
Expected Behaviour: All-time person-days aggregated by resource function/industry; matrix uses current-month FTE.

Feature: Location analysis & saturation trend & cost forecast
User Story: As a manager, I want per-location utilization, a per-resource 10-month saturation trend, and a 4-month cost forecast so that I can plan.
Expected Behaviour: Location: current month utilization = allocated/available; trend: -6..+3 months for selected resource; forecast compares 6-month historic average vs next months' committed cost.

User Story: As a manager, I want revenue by industry, revenue mix T&M vs Fixed, billing pipeline and top margin projects so that I can monitor revenue.
Expected Behaviour: RevenueByIndustry uses sell rates from contract rate cards (current month); RevenueMix per month of current year (T&M from allocations, Fixed from milestones); pipeline sums PLANNED milestones next 6 months; TopMargin = revenue-cost current month top 10.

User Story: As a manager, I want WBS saturation and expiring contracts so that I can renew in time.
Expected Behaviour: WbsSaturation: top 5 contracts with saturation >80% (consumed=capienza-backlog); ContractExpirations: contracts ending within 90 days sorted by date; NoWbsLeakage: current-month cost on assignments without contract/WBS with navigation.

User Story: As a manager, I want a read-only per-resource load grid (day/week/month) so that I can spot over/under-load without editing.
Expected Behaviour: Same color semantics as staffing; day view 14 days with leave icons; week/month views 12 averaged columns; resource links to /staffing?resourceId=...; resigned resources excluded.

User Story: As a manager, I want quick filters Tutte/Sottostaffate/Sovrastaffate/Anomalie so that I can isolate problem resources.
Expected Behaviour: UNDER: avg<max; OVER: avg>max; ISSUES: avg≠max computed over the visible period; combined with resource/role filters; Reset clears all.

User Story: As a manager, I want Excel and PDF export of the workload so that I can share it.
Expected Behaviour: Excel export includes resource, role, location, function, avg load, max %; PDF includes top-10 bar chart, load distribution doughnut and detail table.

User Story: As a restricted user, I should see a lock message if I lack 'allocations' visibility so that data stays protected.
Expected Behaviour: If hasEntityVisibility('allocations') is false, page shows lock icon + message instead of data.

User Story: As a PM, I want a Gantt timeline of projects colored by status so that I can monitor schedules.
Expected Behaviour: Bars positioned by start/end dates; color by status (In corso=primary, Completato=tertiary, In pausa=yellow); today line shown; timescale spans all projects and always includes today.

User Story: As a PM, I want month/quarter/year zoom, name/client filters and date sort so that I can adjust the view.
Expected Behaviour: Zoom rebuilds column scale; name search is case-insensitive substring; client select filters; sort toggles by startDate asc/desc; Reset clears filters.

User Story: As a PM, I want to expand a project row to see assigned resources so that I know who works on it.
Expected Behaviour: Chevron appears only if project has assignments; expanding lists resource names as sub-rows.

User Story: As a PM, I want to export the filtered project list so that I can share timelines.
Expected Behaviour: Export contains name, client, status, formatted start/end dates for filtered rows.

User Story: As a resource manager, I want a 12-month utilization forecast so that I can anticipate capacity issues.
Expected Behaviour: For each month: available person-days = working days (net of approved leaves, hire/last day, max%) ; allocated = real allocations; utilization %; surplus/deficit; bar chart with >100% red, >90% yellow.

User Story: As a resource manager, I want projected load based on historical averages for future months without hard bookings so that the forecast is realistic.
Expected Behaviour: Toggle 'Includi Proiezioni Future' ON by default; for future months without allocations, projects the historical average % over project-bounded working days; projected months marked '(Proiezione)' and striped in chart.

User Story: As a resource manager, I want function/client/project filters so that I can forecast a subset.
Expected Behaviour: Client change resets project filter; project filter restricts resources to those assigned; Reset clears all.

User Story: As an HR manager, I want KPIs (active, resigned, bench, avg cost) so that I see workforce status; clicking active/resigned filters the table.
Expected Behaviour: Cards computed from data; active/resigned cards act as status filter buttons with pressed state.

User Story: As an HR manager, I want a sortable table with name/role/function/industry/location/status filters and an 'only unassigned' toggle so that I can find people.
Expected Behaviour: Filters debounced 400ms; status default 'active'; 'Solo non allocate' shows only resources with no assignments; table shows role, cost (resource override or role cost), location, people lead, status, last day, avg allocation (current month), active projects, seniority years.

User Story: As an HR manager, I want a detailed form with validation so that resource data stays consistent.
Expected Behaviour: Zod validation: name≥3 chars, email required, role/function/location required, lastDayOfWork ≥ hireDate; resigned checkbox reveals required last-day field and clears it when unchecked; max staffing slider 0-100 step 5; talent flag; seniority code; tutor (cannot be self); failure shows toast 'Controlla i dati inseriti.' and field errors.

User Story: As an HR manager, I want to add/remove skills with level and dates in the resource form so that competencies stay current.
Expected Behaviour: Skill picker excludes already-added skills; each entry has level (1-5 labels), acquisition and expiration dates; save syncs additions/updates/removals via resource_skills.

User Story: As an HR manager, I want inline row editing for common fields so that I can fix data fast.
Expected Behaviour: Quick edit turns row into inputs (name, role, cost, location, tutor); check saves with spinner, X cancels.

User Story: As an HR manager, I want to delete a resource with its dependent data so that no orphans remain.
Expected Behaviour: deleteResource cascades (assignments/allocations etc. via useCascadeOps); spinner while deleting.

User Story: As a user, I want URL params ?filter=unassigned and ?editId=<id> and candidate prefill so that flows link into this page.
Expected Behaviour: filter=unassigned enables the toggle; editId opens edit modal for that resource; router-state prefillResource opens pre-filled 'new resource' form (from interviews hire flow).

User Story: As an HR manager, I want Excel/PDF export of filtered resources so that I can report headcount.
Expected Behaviour: Excel has full column set; PDF has function doughnut, location bar chart and list table.

User Story: As a restricted user, I should see a lock message if I lack 'resources' visibility.
Expected Behaviour: Lock icon + message replace content when hasEntityVisibility('resources') is false.

User Story: As a PM, I want KPIs (active projects, total budget, ending within 30 days) so that I see portfolio health.
Expected Behaviour: Cards computed from 'In corso' projects; endingSoon counts active projects with endDate within next 30 days.

User Story: As a PM, I want name/client/status filters and a 'Solo senza staff' toggle so that I find projects fast.
Expected Behaviour: Debounced 400ms; unstaffed toggle shows only 'In corso' projects without assignments; 'Senza Staff' badge shown on such rows; sortable columns; row shows client, assigned resource count, status badge, start date, budget.

User Story: As a PM, I want a project form with validation so that project data is consistent.
Expected Behaviour: Zod: name required, budget ≥ 0, realization 0-200%, endDate ≥ startDate; sections Dati Base / Pianificazione & Budget / Associazioni; contract link, required-skills multi-select, notes; on create the new id is kept so retry after skill error doesn't duplicate the project.

User Story: As a PM, I want to quick-edit name/client/budget/status/start date inline so that fixes are fast.
Expected Behaviour: Row switches to inputs; check saves (optimistic-lock version preserved), X cancels.

User Story: As a PM, I want to delete a project with its assignments/allocations so that data stays clean.
Expected Behaviour: deleteProject cascades via useCascadeOps; spinner during deletion.

User Story: As a PM, I want to set the billing type and manage fixed-price milestones so that revenue is planned.
Expected Behaviour: Modal shows billing type select (T&M/Fixed Price) with save button appearing only when changed; T&M shows linked contract+rate card info or a warning if no contract; Fixed Price shows milestone list (name, date, amount, status PLANNED/INVOICED/PAID editable inline), add-milestone form, delete, and total vs budget (red if over).

User Story: As a PM, I want to record extra project expenses so that costs are complete.
Expected Behaviour: Modal lists expenses sorted by date desc with total and % of budget; add form (category, description, date, amount, billable flag); delete asks confirmation; toasts on success/error.

User Story: As a user, I want ?projectId, ?clientId, ?filter=unstaffed, ?editId params so that other pages can link here.
Expected Behaviour: projectId sets name filter to that project's name; clientId sets client filter; filter=unstaffed sets toggle; editId opens edit modal; params are cleared after applying.

User Story: As a PM, I want Excel/PDF export of the filtered projects so that I can report.
Expected Behaviour: Excel with full fields; PDF with status doughnut, top-10 budget bar and list table.

User Story: As a restricted user, I should see a lock message if I lack 'projects' visibility.
Expected Behaviour: Lock icon + message instead of content.

User Story: As an account manager, I want totals (clients, active clients, sectors) so that I see the client base.
Expected Behaviour: Active = clients with at least one 'In corso' project; sectors = distinct non-empty sectors.

User Story: As an account manager, I want to list/filter/add/edit/delete clients so that the registry stays current.
Expected Behaviour: Name + sector filters; add/edit modal (name*, sector select, contactEmail*); inline quick edit; delete with spinner; deep link ?editId opens edit modal.

User Story: As an account manager, I want Excel/PDF export so that I can share the client list.
Expected Behaviour: Excel (name, sector, email); PDF with sector doughnut and top-10 clients by project count.

User Story: As an admin, I want roles with daily/standard cost, overhead % and activity mix so that costing is accurate.
Expected Behaviour: Add/edit modal with name*, seniority level, dailyCost*, standardCost, overheadPct (shows computed € = dailyCost×pct), mix Chargeable/Training/BD with live bar; submit blocked unless mix sums to 100% (error shown); name/seniority filters; export.

User Story: As an admin, I want to quick-edit name/seniority/cost/overhead inline so that updates are fast.
Expected Behaviour: Row becomes editable; save/cancel buttons with spinner.

User Story: As an admin, I want a confirm dialog warning that delete fails if role is in use so that I don't break resources.
Expected Behaviour: ConfirmationModal warns; success toast on deletion; context toast on failure.

User Story: As a competence manager, I want KPIs (total, top macro area, unused skills) with the unused card acting as a filter so that I spot dead entries.
Expected Behaviour: Cards computed from non-certification skills; clicking 'Inutilizzate' toggles unusedOnly filter (ring highlight).

User Story: As a competence manager, I want to create/edit/delete skills with category multi-select so that the catalog stays clean.
Expected Behaviour: Modal with name* and Ambiti multi-select; version preserved on update; delete confirmation states how many resources/projects lose the skill; deep link ?editId opens editor.

User Story: As a competence manager, I want name/macro/ambito filters and a table↔card toggle so that I browse comfortably.
Expected Behaviour: Filters combine; card view shows usage counters; table sortable with usage columns.

User Story: As a competence manager, I want to assign one or many skills to many resources at once so that onboarding is fast.
Expected Behaviour: Row person_add pre-selects that skill; header 'Assegna' opens bulk modal with skills+resources multi-selects, level and dates; submit requires ≥1 resource and ≥1 skill; N associations saved with success toast.

User Story: As a competence manager, I want Excel/PDF exports so that I can share the catalog.
Expected Behaviour: Excel with usage counts; PDF top-10 charts + table.

User Story: As an HR manager, I want certifications managed separately (isCertification=true) with expiry KPIs so that renewals are tracked.
Expected Behaviour: KPIs: types, expiring within 90 days, top macro area; CRUD like skills with 'verified' badge and info note; delete confirms removal from resources; ?editId deep link.

User Story: As an HR manager, I want per-cert expiring counts and assignment to resources with dates so that I renew in time.
Expected Behaviour: Rows show warning badge when some resource's expiration is within 90 days; person_add opens assignment modal (default level 5-expert, acquisition/expiration dates).

User Story: As an HR manager, I want Excel/PDF exports so that I can report certification coverage.
Expected Behaviour: Excel with expiring column; PDF top-10 bar + valid vs expiring pie.

User Story: As a user, I should only see leave requests I'm entitled to see so that privacy is preserved.
Expected Behaviour: ADMIN sees all; users see their own; approvers see requests assigned to them; non-SIMPLE roles see all APPROVED requests; SIMPLE users only their own.

User Story: As a manager, I want Pending/Approved/Rejected/OnLeaveToday KPIs where the first three toggle filtering so that I can triage.
Expected Behaviour: Clicking a KPI toggles that status filter (highlight + scale); Assenti Oggi = approved requests covering today.

User Story: As an employee, I want to request leave with type, dates, half-day, approvers and notes so that my manager can approve it.
Expected Behaviour: Zod: resource, type, start, end required, end ≥ start; approvers multi-select limited to manager resources; on create a notification is sent to each approver with link /#/leaves; errors shown inline.

User Story: As an approver, I want quick approve/reject from the row and from the modal so that handling is fast.
Expected Behaviour: Buttons visible when status=PENDING and user is admin, or non-owner (non-SIMPLE or designated approver); approval sets managerId to the actor; modal shows Approva/Rifiuta buttons under same rules.

User Story: As a requester, I can edit/delete only my PENDING requests (admin can always) so that approved history stays intact.
Expected Behaviour: Edit/delete icons only for admin or owner+PENDING; delete uses confirmation modal explaining capacity restoration.

User Story: As a manager, I want a monthly team calendar of absences so that I see coverage.
Expected Behaviour: 6-week UTC grid Mon-Sun; leaves (not REJECTED) rendered as chips colored by type, dashed border if PENDING, ½ marker for half days; holidays/weekends highlighted; Prev/Today/Next month navigation; clicking a chip opens edit modal only if permitted.

User Story: As a manager, I want resource/type filters and Excel/PDF export so that I can report absences.
Expected Behaviour: Filters combine with status KPI; export includes duration in working days (0.5 for half day) and approver names; PDF has status doughnut and type bar.

User Story: As an admin, I want to manage holidays and closures so that working-day math is correct everywhere.
Expected Behaviour: DataTable sorted by date with name/type filters; add/edit modal (name*, date*, type NATIONAL_HOLIDAY/COMPANY_CLOSURE/LOCAL_HOLIDAY); location field appears only for LOCAL_HOLIDAY and is cleared otherwise; delete with spinner.

User Story: As an admin, I want to manage functions, industries, seniority levels, project statuses, client sectors and locations so that dropdowns stay current.
Expected Behaviour: Six ConfigSection cards each with list + Aggiungi/edit modal (single 'Valore' field) and delete; errors show toast.

User Story: As an admin, I want to manage skill macro-areas and categories (with macro links) so that skills are classified.
Expected Behaviour: Macro Ambiti list CRUD; Ambiti list CRUD with macroCategoryIds multi-select shown as subtitle.

User Story: As a sales ops user, I want to create rate cards and edit per-resource sell rates so that revenue is computed correctly.
Expected Behaviour: Left panel lists rate cards (auto-select first, add/edit/delete with confirmation noting entries loss); right panel groups active resources by role with reference internal cost (resource-specific > role standard > role daily, * marks specific), editable sell rate, live margin % badge (red<0, yellow<20, green otherwise).

User Story: As a sales ops user, I want to initialize sell rates as cost+20% and save all changes at once so that setup is fast.
Expected Behaviour: 'Inizializza' asks confirm then fills rates=basecost×1.2 for visible (role-filtered) resources with toast count; 'Salva Modifiche' button appears only when there are unsaved changes and upserts all entries.

User Story: As a user, I want a role filter and rate_cards visibility enforcement.
Expected Behaviour: Role select filters the grid; lock screen without 'rate_cards' visibility.

User Story: As an admin, I want to import entities from an Excel template so that I can load data massively.
Expected Behaviour: 3-step flow: choose type (8 types; Utenti e Permessi admin-only), download generated template, upload .xlsx/.xls and import; parses expected sheets per type and POSTs to /api/import; success message with warnings list; failure shows error; app data refetched after import.

User Story: As a user, I want one-click Excel exports per domain so that I can back up or edit data offline.
Expected Behaviour: Cards: monthly allocations (3 months), core entities, staffing grid, resource requests, interviews, skills+associations, leaves, tutor mapping, and (admin only) users & permissions (no passwords); spinner per card during export; alert on failure.

User Story: As a controller, I want per-project labor cost, expenses, T&M revenue, margin and budget variance so that I can assess profitability.
Expected Behaviour: Tab 'Costi e Margini': cost=allocations×historical role cost; revenue=allocations×sell rate from contract rate card; margin badge colored (≥30 green, >0 yellow, else red); client/status filters; CSV + Excel + PDF export.

User Story: As a controller, I want monthly per-resource availability, allocation, utilization % and allocated cost so that I can review usage.
Expected Behaviour: Month picker (default current); role/function filters; availability accounts for hire date, last day, max staffing %, holidays per location; CSV/Excel/PDF export.

User Story: As a controller, I want to switch between the two reports so that both analyses are one click away.
Expected Behaviour: Pill tabs 'Costi e Margini' / 'Utilizzo Risorse' swap content with fade-in.

User Story: As a contract manager, I want totals (contracts, capienza, backlog with %) so that I see financial coverage.
Expected Behaviour: Cards computed with numeric parsing; backlog % = backlog/capienza.

User Story: As a contract manager, I want contracts with CIG, WBS, capienza, rate card, billing type, dates and linked projects/managers so that agreements are tracked.
Expected Behaviour: Zod: name*, cig*, capienza ≥ 0, endDate ≥ startDate; billingType inherited by projects; multi-select links to projects and manager resources persisted as join rows; deep link ?editId.

User Story: As a contract manager, I want a per-row 'Ricalcola Backlog' action so that consumption is refreshed from allocations.
Expected Behaviour: Refresh icon triggers recalculateContractBacklog with spinner; backlog shown red when negative.

User Story: As a contract manager, I want name/CIG/WBS text filters and Excel/PDF export.
Expected Behaviour: Filters combine (case-insensitive substring); export includes joins and formatted values; PDF billing-type pie + top-10 capienza/backlog bars.

User Story: As a controller, I want allocations grouped WBS → Project → Resource over months so that I audit where effort goes.
Expected Behaviour: Expandable tree rows; monthly columns; totals per row; unit switch Days/FTE/Cost; sticky first column.

User Story: As a controller, I want to change unit, period and filter, and export the aggregation.
Expected Behaviour: Unit selector days/fte/cost; export button; entity-visibility guard for restricted users.

User Story: As a CFO, I want a yearly D3 chart of revenue, cost and margin % so that I see financial trends.
Expected Behaviour: For selected year: T&M revenue = allocations×sell rate (contract rate card); Fixed Price revenue = milestones amounts; cost = allocations×historical cost×realization; bars+line chart theme-aware; year selector.

User Story: As a CFO, I want overview/WBS/client/project breakdown tabs with client/project/WBS filters so that I can drill into revenue.
Expected Behaviour: Tabs switch aggregation dimension; filters cascade (project options limited by client); reset button; DataTable with export.

User Story: As a staffing manager, I want resource requests with auto-generated HCR codes and rich validation so that demand is tracked.
Expected Behaviour: Zod: project*, role*, dates* (end≥start), commitment 0-100, OSR number required when isOsrOpen; isLongTerm auto-set when period >60 days; status ATTIVA/STANDBY/CHIUSA with badges; new requests get sequential HCR##### code; deep link ?editId.

User Story: As a staffing manager, I want a best-fit search for a request so that I can find available internal resources.
Expected Behaviour: Row action opens matching modal (blocked with warning for CHIUSA requests); getBestFitResources scores candidates on availability/role/skills and shows score bars; errors show toast.

User Story: As a staffing manager, I want FTE-by-role and requests-by-project summaries for open requests so that I see aggregate demand.
Expected Behaviour: Summary excludes STANDBY and CHIUSA; FTE = sum(commitment)/100 per role sorted desc.

User Story: As a staffing manager, I want project/role/status/requestor filters and Excel/PDF export.
Expected Behaviour: Filters combine; export includes flags (urgent/tech/OSR); visibility guard for 'resource_requests'.

User Story: As a recruiter, I want candidate interviews with ratings, feedback, hiring status and linked resource request so that recruiting is tracked.
Expected Behaviour: Form with candidate data, function, role, CV summary, interviewers multi-select, date, 8 rating dimensions (avg shown as stars), feedback, status Aperto/StandBy/Chiuso, hiring status; version preserved; delete with confirmation; ?editId deep link.

User Story: As a recruiter, I want KPIs (open, positive feedback, hired/offer, standby, next incoming) so that I see the pipeline health.
Expected Behaviour: Cards computed from interviews; 'incoming' = earliest entryDate among hired.

User Story: As a recruiter, I want to create a resource from a hired candidate and close the linked request so that onboarding starts clean.
Expected Behaviour: When hiringStatus=SI, entry date field and 'create resource' button appear; navigates to /resources with prefilled form (name, role, function, hireDate, notes); linked ATTIVA request can be closed with one click and toast.

User Story: As a recruiter, I want name/role/status/feedback filters and Excel/PDF export.
Expected Behaviour: Filters combine; export includes average rating; entity-visibility guard for 'interviews'.

User Story: As a competence manager, I want a per-resource view of skills/certifications with expiry warnings so that I see coverage per person.
Expected Behaviour: Active resources enriched with role, primary skill, dominant category/macro, counts of skills/certifications and certifications expiring ≤90 days (expired highlighted); table↔card views.

Feature: Skill map filters & inline skill editing
User Story: As a competence manager, I want resource/role/skill/category filters and per-resource skill editing so that data upkeep is easy.
Expected Behaviour: Filters incl. displayMode (all/skills only/certs only/empty); edit modal manages the resource's skill list with level and dates using addResourceSkill/deleteResourceSkill.

User Story: As a competence manager, I want a D3 network of resources↔skills so that I can explore relationships visually.
Expected Behaviour: Network view builds nodes for filtered skills+resources and links from resourceSkills; zoom in/out/reset buttons; theme-aware colors.

User Story: As a competence manager, I want alternative charts (radar, sankey, bubble) so that I analyze skill distribution from multiple angles.
Expected Behaviour: View selector switches chart type; charts derive from same filters (resources, roles, skills, category, macro, location, display mode, hide-empty toggle).

User Story: As a manager, I want a visual org chart of users (multi-parent aware) so that I understand reporting lines.
Expected Behaviour: Nodes colored by role with badges; SIMPLE users auto-placed under PMs of their assigned projects (marked auto-placed); expandable/collapsible nodes; inactive users faded; search highlight; data via authorized app-users fetch.

User Story: As a people lead, I want a per-resource timeline of yearly evaluations with metrics so that growth is documented.
Expected Behaviour: Select resource → chronological cards with fiscal year, evaluator, status (Bozza/Completata/Archiviata), summary, metric rows (category, value, score/5) and overall rating/100.

User Story: As a people lead, I want to add/edit/delete evaluations only when authorized so that reviews are controlled.
Expected Behaviour: Edit/delete only when canEdit (admin/evaluator rules); confirmation modal on delete; metrics grouped in categories Obiettivi/Competenze/Soft Skills/Feedback.

User Story: As a manager, I want a monthly Sankey (Resource→Project→Client/Contract) and network view so that I see staffing flows.
Expected Behaviour: Month selector (±6 months); links weighted by person-days in month (excludes weekends/holidays); view toggle sankey/network; theme-aware D3 rendering.

User Story: As a planner, I want a sandbox copy of resources/projects/staffing so that I can simulate changes without touching real data.
Expected Behaviour: Simulation state held in a reducer; tabs Configurazione/Staffing/Analisi; start from real data; ghost resources can be added with role/function/location and financials; per-project expenses and billing modals.

User Story: As a planner, I want to save and load named scenarios so that simulations persist.
Expected Behaviour: Scenarios persisted via analytics_cache API (scope SIMULATION) with name/description/updatedAt; load modal lists them; save modal names them.

User Story: As a planner, I want financial analysis of the scenario and Excel export/import so that I can share simulations.
Expected Behaviour: Analysis tab computes costs/revenue/margin using sim financials and rate cards (bulk apply rate card supported); export builds multi-sheet Excel (Info, Resources, Projects, Assignments...); import restores from file.

User Story: As a user, I want a full-page search across resources, projects, clients, skills etc. so that I find anything fast.
Expected Behaviour: Min 2 chars; results grouped by type with counts and highlighted matches; clicking navigates to the entity deep link; clear button resets.

User Story: As a user, I want KB articles with HTML (WYSIWYG) or plain text content, tags and linked entities so that knowledge is centralized.
Expected Behaviour: List sorted by updatedAt desc; create/edit modal with title, format switch (RichTextEditor for html, monospace textarea for plain), comma-separated tags, EntityLinkPicker for resources/skills/projects/contracts/clients; delete with confirmation; ?openId deep link opens an article.

User Story: As a user, I want text search and format/entity-type filters so that I find articles quickly.
Expected Behaviour: searchArticles matches title/content/tags; filters by format and linked entity type; empty state shown when none.

User Story: As a user, I want an inbox of notifications with unread filter and mark-as-read so that I don't miss approvals.
Expected Behaviour: Default filter 'Da Leggere'; unread count in header; clicking marks read and navigates to notification link; 'Segna tutte come lette' bulk action; icons inferred from title; relative dates (time/Ieri/date).

User Story: As an admin, I want CRUD of simple event→webhook configs so that Teams gets notified on events.
Expected Behaviour: Tab 'Webhook Semplici': table of notification_configs with event type (22 types), URL, enabled; create/edit modal; delete confirmation; test send returns simulated success in mock mode.

User Story: As an admin, I want to compose Adaptive Card notifications with drag-and-drop blocks so that messages are customized.
Expected Behaviour: Tab 'Builder Avanzato' edits notification_rules; palette blocks: header, facts, detailed facts, text, image (QuickChart), table, divider; blocks reorderable; color themes; preview; save rule.

User Story: As an admin, I want to configure months of allocations loaded before/after today so that performance is tuned.
Expected Behaviour: monthsBefore 0-24, monthsAfter 1-36; 'Salva su DB & Ricarica' appears only when changed; persists globally.

User Story: As an admin, I want to bulk edit talent flags and seniority codes so that grading is fast.
Expected Behaviour: Searchable table of active resources with inline checkbox and code input; changes staged locally and saved in one bulk action with toast.

User Story: As an admin, I want to configure up to 8 Cmd+K quick actions so that the search palette fits our workflows.
Expected Behaviour: Add (max 8 with warning), edit label/icon/link/color inline, reorder with up/down, remove; 'Salva su DB' appears only when list differs from saved.

User Story: As an admin, I want a security control panel with pillars (Audit, RBAC matrix, Entity visibility, Identity, Hierarchy, Navigation) so that all security config is in one place.
Expected Behaviour: Left pillar menu switches content; default pillar Audit; admin-only route.

User Story: As an admin, I want to manage app users (whitelist, roles, impersonation, password resets) so that access is controlled.
Expected Behaviour: IdentityPillar lists app-users with role editing and impersonate action (mock returns target user token and reloads data, navigating home).

User Story: As an admin, I want per-role route permissions, per-role entity visibility toggles and menu/landing configuration so that roles see only what they should.
Expected Behaviour: RBACPillar edits route×role matrix; EntityVisibilityPillar bulk-saves role_entity_visibility rules (non-admin roles then get filtered data); NavigationPillar configures sidebar sections and default home per role; AuditPillar shows activity timeline.

User Story: As an admin, I want to browse tables, edit rows inline and truncate tables so that I can fix data directly.
Expected Behaviour: Table list auto-loads (first selected); rows shown with columns; row edit and save; 'delete all rows' with confirmation modal; 401/403 shows session-expired toast.

User Story: As an admin, I want to run raw SQL, export PG/MySQL dumps and bulk-reset passwords so that maintenance is possible in-app.
Expected Behaviour: Query mode textarea + run (mock returns unsupported notice); export buttons download SQL dumps via authorized fetch; bulk password mode parses username:password lines (min 8 chars) and reports success/fail counts.

User Story: As a user, I want a login form with client-side validation so that I get immediate feedback.
Expected Behaviour: Zod: username ≥3 chars, password ≥8; field errors under inputs with helper texts; API error shown in red banner; spinner during login; success stores token+user and redirects; if protection disabled a yellow banner explains it; authenticated users are redirected away.

User Story: As a user, I want an in-app manual and a simple leave guide so that I can learn the app.
Expected Behaviour: Static content pages /manuale-utente and /simple-user-manual reachable from sidebar 'Supporto' section.
